name: QuickBooks
one_liner: Intuit's QuickBooks Online exposes a REST Accounting API covering dozens of accounting entities (invoices, customers, bills, payments, reports) via OAuth 2.0, with a free sandbox and an official Intuit-published MCP server.
access_notes: Anyone can create a free Intuit Developer account, register an app, and get a Client ID/Secret plus a free QuickBooks Online sandbox company that returns working API credentials with no payment or human approval. Production use hits a real QBO company but the sandbox path is fully self-serve. OAuth 2.0 Playground lets you mint tokens without writing code.
api_notes: REST Accounting API with CRUD across dozens of entity types (Account, Customer, Vendor, Invoice, Bill, Payment, Estimate, Item, JournalEntry, etc.) plus a query language and financial reports; well over 100 operations total. Also separate Payments and Payroll APIs. Access tokens expire hourly and must be refreshed.
mcp_notes: Intuit publishes an official open-source local MCP server at github.com/intuit/quickbooks-online-mcp-server (Early Preview, announced Oct 2025 on the Intuit Developer blog). ~20+ tools over core accounting entities and reports; runs locally, authenticates via your own Intuit Developer app OAuth credentials, currently sandbox-focused. Multiple community MCP servers also exist.
main_blocker: None for building/testing: free dev account + sandbox gives working OAuth credentials. Minor friction is OAuth2 authorization-code flow with hourly token refresh, and moving to production requires connecting a real QBO company.

evidence.claim: QuickBooks Online API uses OAuth 2.0 authorization code flow; app registration on Intuit Developer yields Client ID/Secret, and an OAuth 2.0 Playground generates tokens.
Intuit announced an official local QuickBooks Online MCP Server (Early Preview) built on the QBO Accounting APIs.
Intuit hosts the official quickbooks-online-mcp-server repository exposing QBO features as MCP tools.

open_questions: Exact production-access review requirements for publishing a public app to Intuit's app store (distinct from raw API use), and current stable-vs-preview status of the official MCP server beyond sandbox.
